name: Phrase Blend
description: Smooth 16/32-bar phrase-aligned blend.
execution: Quantize to phrase boundary if available.
Apply phrase blend gesture with wet/dry automation.
Return FX to neutral before next phrase unless marked as release FX.
guardrails: Do not stack more than two dominant vocals.
Keep limiter headroom at least -6 dB before transition.
Prefer preview rendering before live execution.

execution: Quantize to phrase boundary if available.

guardrails: Do not stack more than two dominant vocals.

execution: Quantize to phrase boundary if available.

guardrails: Do not stack more than two dominant vocals.

description: Move between halftime/doubletime feel.
execution: Quantize to phrase boundary if available.
Apply half-time flip gesture with wet/dry automation.
Return FX to neutral before next phrase unless marked as release FX.
guardrails: Do not stack more than two dominant vocals.
Keep limiter headroom at least -6 dB before transition.

name: Key Lift Transition
description: Compatible upward key move for brighter energy.
execution: Quantize to phrase boundary if available.
Apply key lift transition gesture with wet/dry automation.
Return FX to neutral before next phrase unless marked as release FX.
guardrails: Do not stack more than two dominant vocals.
Keep limiter headroom at least -6 dB before transition.
Prefer preview rendering before live execution.

name: Energy Dip Reset
description: Brief energy drop to make next drop bigger.
execution: Quantize to phrase boundary if available.
Apply energy dip reset gesture with wet/dry automation.
Return FX to neutral before next phrase unless marked as release FX.
guardrails: Do not stack more than two dominant vocals.
Keep limiter headroom at least -6 dB before transition.
Prefer preview rendering before live execution.

name: Reverb Wash Reset
description: Use a short reverb bloom to mask a key or tempo reset before revealing the next track.
execution: Start a controlled reverb wash on the outgoing deck.
Cut outgoing low end before the wash gets dense.
Fade the incoming deck through the wash and release FX before the next phrase.
guardrails: Do not stack more than two dominant vocals.
Keep limiter headroom at least -6 dB before transition.
Prefer preview rendering before live execution.

name: Low Kill Drop
description: Kill the outgoing low end early and let the incoming drop take bass ownership.
execution: Quantize the entry to a phrase or drop boundary.
Kill A low immediately before B low opens.
Use a short crossfade while keeping mids controlled.
guardrails: Do not stack more than two dominant vocals.
Keep limiter headroom at least -6 dB before transition.
Prefer preview rendering before live execution.

name: Drop Swap Cut
description: Hold the outgoing phrase, then cut directly into the incoming drop without release FX.
execution: Keep A dominant until the phrase edge.
Preload B at the drop or hook cue.
Cut the crossfader late and stop A cleanly.
guardrails: Do not stack more than two dominant vocals.
Keep limiter headroom at least -6 dB before transition.
Prefer preview rendering before live execution.

name: Energy Dip Reset Plus
description: Create a short perceived energy dip to make an otherwise risky tempo or key move feel intentional.
execution: Reduce outgoing low end and channel level briefly.
Hold the dip for less than one phrase.
Bring B in with restored low end after the reset.
guardrails: Do not stack more than two dominant vocals.
Keep limiter headroom at least -6 dB before transition.
Prefer preview rendering before live execution.

name: Percussion Bridge Plus
description: Layer a percussive intro or loop over the outgoing track before a late bass handoff.
execution: Start B with low killed and high percussion audible.
Keep both decks near center while groove locks.
Swap low end late and fade A by channel fader.
guardrails: Do not stack more than two dominant vocals.
Keep limiter headroom at least -6 dB before transition.
Prefer preview rendering before live execution.

name: Stutter Gate Exit
description: Use a rhythmic gate or roll-style chop to exit a busy phrase without a long overlap.
execution: Arm a short beat-synced gate on A.
Reduce A low end before the final chop.
Reveal B on the next downbeat and release the gate.
guardrails: Do not stack more than two dominant vocals.
Keep limiter headroom at least -6 dB before transition.
Prefer preview rendering before live execution.

name: Dub Delay Bridge
description: Throw outgoing percussion or vocal fragments into delay while the incoming groove takes over.
execution: Apply a tempo-matched delay throw to A accents.
Keep feedback moderate and remove A low end.
Fade B in under the delay tail.
guardrails: Do not stack more than two dominant vocals.
Keep limiter headroom at least -6 dB before transition.
Prefer preview rendering before live execution.

name: Mid Carve Blend
description: Protect vocal and lead clarity by dynamically carving the outgoing midrange during overlap.
execution: Start B with low killed and modest high end.
Duck A mids only while B vocal or lead is present.
Return or fade A before the next hook.
guardrails: Do not stack more than two dominant vocals.
Keep limiter headroom at least -6 dB before transition.
Prefer preview rendering before live execution.

name: Ambient Break Wash
description: Blend two low-energy break sections with gentle filtering and restrained space.
execution: Choose break or intro cues on both decks.
Use gentle high-pass filtering on A and low-killed B entry.
Avoid heavy release FX unless the next section needs a reset.
guardrails: Do not stack more than two dominant vocals.
Keep limiter headroom at least -6 dB before transition.
Prefer preview rendering before live execution.

name: Hook Tease Handoff
description: Preview a recognizable hook briefly before committing to the full incoming track.
execution: Tease B hook at low level or with low killed.
Return briefly to A if the phrase needs resolution.
Commit to B on the next phrase boundary.
guardrails: Do not stack more than two dominant vocals.
Keep limiter headroom at least -6 dB before transition.
Prefer preview rendering before live execution.

name: Mid Swap
description: Fade B in with its mid killed, swap both mid bands on a downbeat, then fade A out.

name: High Swap
description: Introduce B without highs, exchange both high bands on a beat, then remove A.

name: Low Initial Cut
description: Run an eight-bar blend with B low killed and swap bass ownership at the midpoint.

name: High Initial Cut
description: Bring B in with highs near -12 dB, exchange highs at the midpoint, and fade A out.

name: V-Curve Fade
description: Cross both channel faders through a controlled 70% midpoint before completing the handoff.

name: Constant Power Fade
description: Use cosine and sine channel curves over sixteen bars to keep the squared gain sum constant.

name: Full-Range Crossfade
description: Linearly exchange channel volume over four bars while leaving all EQ bands flat.

name: Quick Mix
description: Overlay B for four bars, remove A low rapidly, and cut A before the next downbeat.

name: Ghost Drop
description: Create a deliberate silent bar before releasing B at full range on the next downbeat.

name: LPF Dissolve
description: Sweep A through a logarithmic low-pass dissolve while fading B in.

name: Reverb Tail
description: Kill A low, mute its dry channel, and retain a long reverb tail over B.

name: Beat Repeat Roll
description: Shorten a captured beat roll from one beat to one eighth before releasing B.

name: Double Downbeat
description: Repeat the final kick at half-beat length and release B on the following downbeat.

name: Flanger Sweep
description: Increase flanger depth and accelerate its beat-synced cycle before a clean cut to B.

name: Noise Sweep
description: Generate internal white noise, sweep its high-pass filter upward, and shape a rise-and-fall envelope.

name: Gate Transition
description: Apply a quarter-beat gate and reduce duty cycle from continuous audio to short staccato slices.

name: Distortion Fade
description: Raise waveshaper drive with internal makeup attenuation before cutting to a clean B signal.

name: Transformer Cut
description: Apply a sixteenth-beat square gate on the final beat and cut directly to B.

name: Snare Drop
description: Shift execution and the incoming cue by one beat so both tracks hand off on the snare.

name: Break Drop
description: Use a detected break-start exit cue to cut directly into B chorus or drop.

name: Free Run Drop
description: Disable synchronization for large non-ratio BPM changes and cut B in at original tempo.

name: Intro to Outro
description: Start B intro at the detected A outro boundary and perform a long EQ handoff.

name: Chorus to Chorus
description: Cut from an outgoing chorus resolution directly to the incoming chorus downbeat.

name: Ambient Interlude
description: Bridge incompatible tracks with a four-second filtered-noise reset bed.

name: Half / Double BPM
description: Recognize 1:2 tempo ratios, preserve original speed, and lock normalized beat phase.

name: Semitone Shift
description: Prepare the incoming deck with a one-semitone AudioWorklet pitch shift before blending.

name: Drum / Bass Band Swap
description: Frequency-band approximation that exchanges low-end ownership without claiming stem separation.
